danger_signals.visual_form: Dark liquid patches on track surface
danger_signals.meaning: Hazard that causes vehicles to spin out of control
Environmental hazard that slows vehicle movement

danger_signals.visual_form: Sudden vertical obstacles appearing on track
danger_signals.meaning: Crash hazard that damages vehicle if struck
Negative power-up that decreases ammunition count

danger_signals.visual_form: Yellow car sprite moving at extreme speed visibly faster than player
danger_signals.meaning: Indicates opponent car has reached maximum speed (127 mph) due to weapon overuse, cannot be outrun

safety_signals.visual_form: Roll cage icon collectible on track
safety_signals.meaning: Protective power-up that shields vehicle from crash damage
Speed boost zones providing temporary acceleration advantage

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Red truck upgrades to 4-Wheeler, then Off Roader as progression visual
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player always centered in isometric view; other racers positioned relative to player

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Yellow car visibly accelerates to maximum speed at later tracks
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Spawns on track competing for position; most dangerous at track 32

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Twelve unique track layouts with varying hazard distributions; player vehicle as standard red truck
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Opponents are beatable; weapons and power-ups provide meaningful advantage; races are competitive but winnable
Represents unofficial game peak difficulty before mechanic repetition begins

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Repeating track layouts from tracks 1-11 but with additional hazard features overlaid
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty increases through environmental complexity rather than new track design; player vehicle potentially upgraded

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Same track layouts with maximum hazard density; yellow opponent car visually accelerated to max speed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All computer opponents run at maximum speed; player cannot win through racing alone, must use weapons continuously; visual indication that weapons become mandatory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ammunition counter on HUD decreases; visual feedback on weapon availability
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Once weapons exhausted, player is eliminated from race; skull pickups decrease ammunition visibility

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Player truck sprite changes from standard truck to 4-Wheeler to Off Roader as NINTENDO letters collected
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Vehicle performance increases visibly; faster acceleration and top speed as car upgrades through collecting bonus letters

environmental_storytelling.example: Oil slicks, water puddles, and barriers placed strategically on tracks communicate dangerous racing conditions requiring skill and caution
Overhead isometric view shows all four racers simultaneously on track, communicating competitive positioning and relative standing in race at all times
Post-race screen displays collected tune-up items (turbo, engine, tires) visually, showing progression of vehicle capabilities across race series
Spelling 'NINTENDO' through collected letter pickups creates visual progression goal; incomplete spelling communicates incomplete achievement state
Yellow car sprite distinctly colored to identify most dangerous opponent; visual differentiation communicates threat level
Each completed track awards visible trophy; larger 'High Score Trophies' communicate achievement progression and mastery level

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responds to directional input; collects pickups on path; loses to weapons when unprepared; can fire weapons to disable opponents
Visibly faster movement; improved acceleration and top speed performance compared to initial truck
Fastest vehicle option; further improved acceleration and top speed; sprite change signals maximum upgrade state
Races competitively against player; can be defeated with weapons or racing skill; speed matches player capability
Moves visibly faster than player; sprite movement noticeably accelerated; cannot be outpaced by racing alone; indicates weapon use is mandatory
Visually disappear from track when disabled by player weapons; temporary elimination before respawning or race reset
Visibly slowed or stopped momentarily; sprites show damage/stun animation; temporary disable before recovery
Vehicle sprite spins out of control; movement becomes erratic; visual feedback of loss of traction
Vehicle movement visibly slows; sprite velocity decreases; temporary speed penalty display

notes: R.C. Pro-Am uses isometric perspective to communicate racing world state. Visual differentiation through color (red player, yellow dangerous opponent) and sprite changes (truck upgrades to faster vehicles) conveys progression and threat. Hazard visibility (oil slicks, water, barriers) directly communicates danger. The yellow car's visible speed increase at track 32 is a key behavioral tell that world state has shifted to 'unwinnable without weapons.' Weapon pickups and ammunition counter are core manifestations of combat state. Track layout repetition after track 24 with added hazards communicates escalating difficulty through environmental complexity rather than novelty. The HUD elements (track conditions, high score trophies, pause screen) provide quantitative manifestation of game state progression.
